title: Design of an innovative surface-volume dielectric barrier discharge ozone generator for water treatment applications
authors_or_org: Raouti Driss; Kamel Nassour; Bouanane Abdelkrim; Tahtah Abdelkarim; Said Nemmich; Ghaitaoui Essama Ahmed; Amar Tilmatine
claim_supported: Crossref confirms journal-article, Journal of the Korean Physical Society, 2025, 7 named authors. Reports a novel hybrid surface-volume DBD ozone-generator design (cylindrical, stainless-steel-ball high-voltage electrode) for industrial water-treatment ozonation, claimed to outperform conventional volume/surface DBD generators in ozone yield and energy efficiency. Full-text content claim NOT independently confirmed this session beyond bibliographic identity.
notes: New sub-niche: DBD ozone generation for industrial water treatment (distinct from ammonia/CO2/VOC plasma chemistry already in L01-001-029). Author names (Driss, Tilmatine) consistent with Algerian HV/plasma research groups (e.g., Univ. Sidi Bel Abbes/IRECOM) — NOT confirmed by Crossref affiliation data (field was empty); geography left at scout default US pending verifier confirmation. Do not cite performance-comparison claims without a full-text fetch.

title: Atomic Layer Etching of SiO2 Utilizing Ultra-Low Electron Temperature Plasma
authors_or_org: Junyoung Park; Nayeon Kim; Jung-Eun Choi; Yujin Yeo; Min-Seok Kim; Chang-Min Lim; Beom-Jun Seo; Chin-Wook Chung
claim_supported: Crossref confirms journal-article, ACS Applied Electronic Materials, 2025, 8 named authors. Primary research on an ultra-low electron temperature plasma process for atomic layer etching of SiO2, aimed at suppressing plasma-induced substrate damage in leading-edge semiconductor etch. Distinct sub-niche from the RF-impedance-matching-network papers already in the ledger (L01-012/013): this is etch-chemistry/plasma-source physics, not matching-network electronics.
notes: Author-name pattern (Chin-Wook Chung group) consistent with Hanyang University, South Korea plasma-etch research — NOT confirmed by Crossref affiliation data (field empty); geography left at scout default US pending verifier confirmation.

notes: Confirmed as a peer-reviewed IEEE Transactions journal article (Trans. Plasma Science), not merely an IEEE-branded conference paper, satisfying the source-standards IEEE caution. No author affiliation available via Crossref; geography left at scout default US.

title: Plasma Process Metrology
authors_or_org: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) — Chemical Process and Nuclear Measurements Group, Chemical Sciences Division, Material Measurement Laboratory
claim_supported: NIST's Plasma Process Metrology program (Material Measurement Laboratory) develops measurement techniques, reference data, and models to characterize plasma etch/deposition processes for the semiconductor industry, enabling model-based reactor design and process control; named accomplishments include a wave-cutoff-probe electron-density method developed with Korea's KRISS and a validated general model of RF-bias effects on electron density for inductively coupled reactors, plus improved electrical endpoint-detection methods for plasma etching.
locator: Program page body — goals, techniques, and accomplishments sections
notes: New gov/national-metrology sub-niche (plasma measurement science/standards infrastructure underpinning semiconductor plasma-etch tool design) distinct from Sandia's diagnostics facility (L01-045) and Korea's KIMM commercialization lab (L01-047) already in the ledger. No named industrial-partner contract stated on this page.
